title: A Dust-Caked Mystery
lines: Your flashlight beam cuts across the attic's clutter, but it's the sketches that draw your eye again. They aren't just drawings; they're obsessive.
Dozens of papers are pinned to the rafters, showing the same multi-eyed creature in various states of hunger.
You feel a cold sweat on your brow. Whoever lived here wasn't just hiding—they were documenting a nightmare.

options.desc: 1. Look closer at the dates on the papers
3. Open the heavy trunk beneath the sketches

title: The Peel and the Pulse
lines: You press your palm against the loose wallpaper. It doesn't feel like paper; it feels like... skin.
A faint, rhythmic pulse thrums beneath your touch. The wall is breathing, or something behind it is.
A thin, translucent fluid begins to weep from the tears in the wallpaper. It smells like old copper.

options.desc: 1. Pull a large strip of the paper away
2. Stare into the dark gaps and wait
3. Stumble back into the main attic

title: A Sense of Dread
lines: The air in the tunnel is stagnant, but you hear a faint, metallic scraping coming from deep ahead.
Your flashlight flickers once, then twice. The shadows here feel heavy, as if they're leaning in to listen to your heartbeat.
You realize with a start that the scraping sound is perfectly timed with your own breathing.

options.desc: 3. Reach for the handle of the far door
